required_complexity_patterns.notes: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs
Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click
Multiple controls sharing name attribute groups (checkbox/radio clusters)
Label text split across multiple spans with no space between words
Inputs laid out in table cells, not flex card stacks only

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real pracuj-style job application (shadow-dom, large, tier=high). Widgets: date, native-inputs, react-select. Required: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs Required: Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click Required: Multiple controls sharing name attribute groups (checkbox/radio clusters) Required: Label text split across multiple spans with no space between words Required: Inputs laid out in table cells, not flex card stacks only Reference DOM (Other option reveal): Referral source Other checkbox reveals free-text input Reference DOM (Readonly prefilled email): Email input is readonly with prefilled value from profile import Reference DOM (Placeholder-only identification): Empty label element; field identified only by placeholder attribute Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: pill-yes-no, details-accordion, duplicate-name-attrs, glued-label-text, table-layout. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: sidebar + main split apply layout. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Other option reveal; Readonly prefilled email; Placeholder-only identification.
